source_rationale: CD3E is a core T cell receptor complex component, marking this cluster as T cells
CD8A marks cytotoxic T lymphocytes, indicating a CD8+ T cell identity
KLRB1 (CD161) is expressed on NK/T cells, particularly NKT and tissue-resident T cells
Granzyme B is a cytotoxic effector molecule of activated CD8+ T and NK cells
CCL5 (RANTES) is highly expressed by effector/memory T cells
NKG7 is a cytotoxic granule protein expressed in NK and cytotoxic T cells
CD69 is an early activation marker on T cells, suggesting tissue-resident or activated state
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an NK receptor expressed on NK cells and some CD8+ T cells
Interferon-gamma is a hallmark cytokine of activated T cells and NK cells
IL7R (CD127) marks memory T cells and is characteristic of tissue-resident lymphocytes
Alpha-crystallin A is a lens-specific protein, indicating lens epithelial/fiber cells
Beta-crystallin B2 is a major structural protein of the ocular lens
PROX1 is a transcription factor essential for lens fiber cell differentiation
FOXE3 is a transcription factor critical for lens development and maintenance
GJA8 (connexin 50) is a gap junction protein specific to lens fiber cells
Gamma-crystallin S is a lens-specific structural protein

PAX6 is a master regulator of eye development including lens formation

MS4A1 (CD20) is a definitive B cell surface marker

CD79B is the second component of the B cell receptor signaling complex
